menu stuff

Brinjal (Solanum melongena)

Brinjal, known locally as vambatu (Sinhala) and kathirikkai (Tamil), is a warm-season vegetable grown year-round across the low country and mid country of Sri Lanka.

Climatic requirements

  • Elevation: 0 – 1200 m.
  • Temperature: 20 – 30 °C.
  • Rainfall: 600 – 1200 mm.
  • Soil pH: 5.5 – 6.8; well-drained sandy loam preferred.

Cultivation

Crop duration is 90 – 120 days depending on variety. Recommended spacing is 60 cm within row and 90 cm between rows. Seed rate 100 g per acre raised in nursery.

Common released varieties include Thinnaweli Purple and Lena Iri, both suitable for the Low Country Wet Zone (WL1–WL3) and Intermediate Zone.

© Department of Agriculture
